title: Moonshot’s latest Kimi model is so popular that the company just gated new users
excerpt: The Chinese AI startup said it saw massive demand over the last two days and had to pause new subscriptions to reduce strain on its GPUs.
content_html:
One of China's biggest AI players is struggling to cope with its newfound success.

In a Sunday X post, Chinese startup Moonshot AI wrote that its recently released Kimi K3 model has "received far more love" than expected, and its GPUs were feeling the strain.

"Over the past 48 hours, demand has pushed close to the limits of our current capacity," the company wrote. "To protect the experience of existing subscribers, we're temporarily pausing new subscriptions and prioritizing compute for current members."

It added that existing subscribers would not be affected and that it was adding capacity as fast as possible to "reopen new subscription spots in batches."

Moonshot AI announced the launch of Kimi K3 on Friday, calling it "Open Frontier Intelligence" and promising to rival models from Anthropic and OpenAI. The company said Kimi K3 has "frontier intelligence" for tasks like coding, knowledge work, and reasoning.

The company said it plans to release a full open-weight model on July 27. That means developers will be able to download the model's trained parameters and run, modify, or build upon it themselves.

In its X post, Moonshot AI added that it is testing another method to use its compute more effectively — splitting membership tiers into two plans. The two plans are "Kimi Membership for Kimi Web, App, and Work; and Kimi Code Membership for coding workflows."

"This will help us match compute more precisely and keep the experience stable," the company wrote.

Kimi K3's release is the latest indication that the gap between the capabilities of frontier models from Chinese and American AI labs is narrowing. Anthropic and OpenAI have repeatedly called for stronger government regulation to help the US maintain its lead over China in the AI race.

Chinese models are having their moment as more companies see them as cost-effective alternatives to US models. Several US executives, including Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong and Airbnb CEO Brian Chesky, have publicly spoken about their companies' use of models such as Kimi, Z.ai's GLM-5.2, and Alibaba's Qwen.

Read next

Aditi Bharade

content_html_zh:
中国最大的人工智能公司之一正在努力应对新的成功。

在 Sunday X 的一篇帖子中，中国初创公司 Moonshot AI 写道，其最近发布的 Kimi K3 模型“受到了比预期更多的喜爱”，并且其 GPU 感受到了压力。

该公司写道：“过去 48 小时内，需求已接近我们当前产能的极限。” “为了保护现有订阅者的体验，我们暂时暂停新订阅并优先考虑当前会员的计算。”

该公司补充说，现有订阅者不会受到影响，并且正在尽快增加容量，以“批量重新开放新的订阅点”。

Moonshot AI 于周五宣布推出 Kimi K3，称其为“开放前沿智能”，并承诺与 Anthropic 和 OpenAI 的模型相竞争。 该公司表示，Kimi K3 具有编码、知识工作和推理等任务的“前沿智能”。

该公司表示计划于 7 月 27 日发布全开放重量模型。 这意味着开发人员将能够下载模型的训练参数并自行运行、修改或构建。

Moonshot AI 在其 X 帖子中补充说，它正在测试另一种更有效地使用其计算的方法 - 将会员级别分为两个计划。 这两个计划是“Kimi Web、App 和 Work 的 Kimi 会员资格；以及编码工作流程的 Kimi Code 会员资格”。

该公司写道：“这将帮助我们更精确地匹配计算并保持体验稳定。”

Kimi K3的发布是中美人工智能实验室前沿模型能力差距正在缩小的最新迹象。 Anthropic 和 OpenAI 多次呼吁加强政府监管，以帮助美国在人工智能竞赛中保持对中国的领先地位。

随着越来越多的公司将中国模式视为美国模式的具有成本效益的替代品，中国模式正在大放异彩。 包括 Coinbase 首席执行官 Brian Armstrong 和 Airbnb 首席执行官 Brian Chesky 在内的几位美国高管都公开谈论过他们公司使用 Kimi、Z.ai 的 GLM-5.2 和阿里巴巴的 Qwen 等模型。

阅读下一篇
